title: Fix owner principal, surface Labor Rates & Inventory under Dashboard, enforce owner-only access to labor rates and metrics

requirements.text: Update the backend stable owner principal variable to 'w3w26-hrxnk-kfpaw-7trqf-ngcqt-xm3i3-qebzp-vtech-eqcjs-nahkw-fae'. Ensure all hasPermission checks for #owner role use this principal. The authorized user (Ryan) retains #authorized role with CRUD access to clients, jobs, inventory, and other standard operations, but cannot change labor rates or view owner-only metrics.

quotes: That's not my Principal id my principal ID is w3w26-hrxnk-kfpaw-7trqf-ngcqt-xm3i3-qebzp-vtech-eqcjs-nahkw-fae
I'm the owner, he'll be the authorized user.

acceptanceCriteria: The stable owner principal var in main.mo is set to 'w3w26-hrxnk-kfpaw-7trqf-ngcqt-xm3i3-qebzp-vtech-eqcjs-nahkw-fae'.
Calling getCallerRole() with that principal returns #owner.
An authorized user (non-owner principal) cannot call setStripeKey, createLaborRate, updateLaborRate, or deleteLaborRate without receiving an access-denied error.
An authorized user can still call createClient, updateClient, createJob, updateJob, createPart, updatePart, and other #authorized-gated endpoints.

requirements.text: Add a 'Labor Rates' quick-access card or section on the Dashboard page that is only visible to the #owner role. The card should display a summary of existing labor rates (name, type, amount) and include a button that navigates to the Labor Rates management section in Settings. If the caller is not #owner, this section must not render.

quotes: I have full access to change labor rates and view metrics.

acceptanceCriteria: When logged in as the owner principal, the Dashboard shows a 'Labor Rates' card listing current rates fetched via listLaborRates.
The card includes a 'Manage Labor Rates' button that navigates to the Settings page's Labor Rates section.
When logged in as any non-owner principal, the Labor Rates card is completely hidden from the Dashboard.
The card matches the existing green and silver theme.

requirements.text: Add an 'Inventory' quick-access card or section on the Dashboard page visible to both #owner and #authorized roles. The card should display a summary count of total parts, number of low-stock items, and a button that navigates to the full Inventory screen. Low-stock count should be derived from listParts results where quantity <= lowStockThreshold.
acceptanceCriteria: The Dashboard shows an 'Inventory' summary card displaying total part count and low-stock count.
Low-stock count correctly reflects parts where quantity is at or below lowStockThreshold.
The card includes a 'View Inventory' button that navigates to the Inventory screen.
The card is visible to both #owner and #authorized roles.
The card matches the existing green and silver theme.

requirements.text: Ensure the Dashboard metrics section (open jobs count, in-progress jobs count, complete jobs count, total clients count, and any revenue or summary statistics) is only visible to the #owner role. Authorized users navigating to the Dashboard should see jobs and inventory sections but not the owner-only metrics cards.

quotes: I have full access to change labor rates and view metrics.

acceptanceCriteria: When logged in as the owner principal, all metric cards (open jobs, in-progress, complete, total clients) are visible on the Dashboard.
When logged in as an authorized (non-owner) user, the metrics cards are hidden or replaced with a message indicating owner-only access.
The recent jobs list remains visible to #authorized users.
The Inventory summary card (REQ-25) remains visible to #authorized users.

constraints: Owner principal must be exactly 'w3w26-hrxnk-kfpaw-7trqf-ngcqt-xm3i3-qebzp-vtech-eqcjs-nahkw-fae' — do not change it.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor.
Only the #owner role can create, update, or delete labor rates — #authorized role must be blocked from these mutations.
Only the #owner role can view Dashboard metrics cards.
nonGoals: Adding new backend labor rate or inventory endpoints (already implemented).
Changing the labor rates management UI in Settings (already implemented).
Adding blob photo storage, on-screen signatures, or appliance type dropdown in this pass.
Real-time GPS tracking or map integrations beyond existing Google Maps deep-links.
Any email, SMS, or push notification features.
